title: Route protected-surface config ACs to an operator-action disposition — a cycle must never adopt an acceptance criterion that requires an in-cycle write to a control-plane surface it cannot touch (control-plane-blocked-ac class)
summary: cycle-858 (merge-rung1) built all 5 code ACs green but FAILed the whole cycle because scout/TDD, applying phases_are_config_only, dispositioned a registry as a `.evolve/policy.json` config-check (TestC858_004). policy.json is on guards.ProtectedSurfaceManifest (integrity_surface.go), so the role guard denies any phase writing it — the AC is structurally unsatisfiable IN-CYCLE. Two standing rules collide on this one file: phases_are_config_only (put settings in config) vs. the control-plane boundary (a cycle may not edit the surface that grades it). The runtime behavior was safe (compiled fail-closed defaults active), yet a whole pipeline burned on an un-passable predicate — and it re-burns every time the task is re-selected (the loop-blocking-breaker's motivating case). Fix: at scout/TDD acceptance authoring, detect an AC whose satisfaction requires writing a path on the ProtectedSurfaceManifest and route it to a distinct 'operator-action' disposition (stage a proposal artifact + auto-file an operator inbox item) rather than an in-cycle predicate. The cycle then passes on the code ACs and the protected-surface change lands via the sanctioned human-gated ship. Structured failure class already sketched as control-plane-blocked-ac (should quarantine-with-handoff, not count as a plain FAIL).
acceptance: a shared predicate (e.g. guards.IsProtectedSurface(path)) is the SINGLE source both the role guard AND acceptance authoring consult — no second hardcoded list

RED: an AC that would require writing .evolve/policy.json (or any ProtectedSurfaceManifest path) is authored as an operator-action disposition (staged proposal + operator inbox item), NOT an in-cycle predicate; the cycle's remaining code ACs still gate normally
RED: a control-plane-blocked-ac failure quarantines-with-handoff (does not count toward repeat-FAIL burn) rather than recording a plain FAIL
connects_to: loop-blocking-breaker (repeat-FAIL quarantine consumes this failure class)
source: cycle-858 retrospective + the item's own follow-up hypothesis (2026-07-16)
